spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates coarse CPU-tier adaptation with the numeric navigator.cpuPerformance shape plus Client Hints negotiation.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP changed scenario, tier, and delay, then clicked Run adaptive request; status metrics, recipe, and JSON output updated.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and page both describe client/server profile selection using a coarse CPU tier.

edge_cases.rationale: The fallback path works when navigator.cpuPerformance is absent, and the route still returns Accept-CH, Critical-CH, Vary, Server-Timing, and echoed request metadata.

references.rationale: The concept page embeds the ChromeStatus feature link and CPU Performance API draft.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout and keeps form/button color pairs explicit.

summary: Server-side adaptation uses the current numeric CPU-tier helper and no legacy object-shaped fallback. Chrome DevTools MCP verified the capability-echo request returned 200 with expected headers and no console errors.
